rationale: Voss et al. (2003) is cited in the theory development to define and justify the hedonic-versus-utilitarian product distinction and to support claims about expected emotional descriptors across product types. Those claims are used to build key hypotheses (especially expectation differences and backfiring for utilitarian products) and the mechanism around expectation violation and trust. The citing paper also invokes this prior work when motivating its product categorization strategy in empirical analyses. This is therefore a meaningful conceptual input, not a peripheral literature-list citation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Voss et al. (2003) as conceptual groundwork for treating product type (hedonic vs. utilitarian) as a central moderator of emotional review effects. It builds its expectation-based logic and product-type operational framing on that prior hedonic/utilitarian attitude literature.
